operating_state: Full-year 2022 revenue of $28.1 billion increased 23% year-on-year with double-digit growth across all divisions and geographies. International revenue grew broadly with strong performance in Latin America and Europe/CIS/Africa, while North America increased significantly driven by robust onshore drilling activity. Fourth quarter revenue increased 5% sequentially with strong digital sales and Middle East activity inflection as capacity expansion projects mobilized.
cost_structure: Full-year pretax operating margin of 18% expanded 316 basis points driven by improved operating leverage from higher activity, favorable mix, and improving pricing. Fourth-quarter pretax segment operating margin of 19.8% reached highest level since 2015. Working capital consumed $1.7 billion primarily from receivables and inventories increasing to support activity growth. Pension obligations reflect higher discount rates reducing liability valuations but increasing future expense sensitivity.
material_events: SLB suspended new investment and technology deployment to Russia operations in March 2022, with Russia representing approximately 6% of worldwide revenue. Company sold Liberty shares for $732 million proceeds recognizing $325 million gain. SLB increased quarterly dividend 40% to $0.175 per share in April 2022, followed by further 43% increase to $0.25 per share in January 2023. Company repurchased senior notes reducing net debt by $1.7 billion to $9.3 billion.
macro_exposures: Company faces heightened geopolitical risks from Ukraine conflict and related sanctions affecting operations and supply chains. Energy transition acceleration could reduce demand for traditional services if transition occurs faster than anticipated. Climate change regulations may impose taxes, require emissions reductions, or accelerate product obsolescence. Company operates in over 100 tax jurisdictions with evolving regulations creating ongoing audit exposure.
forward_outlook: Management expects strong growth and margin expansion in 2023 based on broad-based international activity acceleration, higher service pricing, and tighter service sector capacity. Global upstream spending projections continue trending positively with activity growth expected across international basins. Capital investments projected at $2.5 to $2.6 billion in 2023 to support continued revenue growth. Company resumed share buyback program under existing authorization following debt optimization.
uncertainty_profile: SLB maintains peso-denominated net assets in Argentina subject to currency controls and parallel exchange rate risk approximately 93% higher than official rate. Company faces potential transaction losses if official rate converges with parallel rate. Sustainability goals and net-zero targets expose company to operational, reputational, and legal risks if targets are not achieved within announced timelines. Goodwill and asset valuations depend on cash flow projections subject to commodity price changes.
